title: reuben device profile
description: The --io-map <file> document (ADR-0038 §6/§7): binds an instrument's logical master channels to a real device, outside the patch, so the same instrument plays on any rig. Every field is optional; an empty document (or omitting --io-map) means identity map + the default device, bit-identical to today's behavior. Hand-maintained (not generated from the operator Registry) — keep in sync with crates/reuben-native/src/profile.rs.

input.description: Applied by the input stream (P5, #182), which opens only when the played instrument binds input channels — an instrument without input pipes never opens an input device. `map` here is device channel -> logical channel, the reverse direction of `output.map`.

sample_rate.description: Preferred output sample rate in Hz, requested against the device's supported configs. The engine adopts whatever the device grants and logs the outcome; omit for the device's own default rate.

buffer_size.description: Preferred output buffer size in frames, clamped into the device's supported range and logged. This is the device callback's buffer size, independent of the core engine's fixed render block size.

device.description: Select a device by case-insensitive name substring. Omit for the host's default device.

map.description: Channel index pairs, JSON-object keys spelled as non-negative integer strings (object keys are always strings). output.map: logical channel -> device channel. input.map: device channel -> logical channel. A pair naming a channel that doesn't exist on the real device is a load-time reality mismatch handled by warn + degrade (dropped for output, zeros for input), never a load error; a non-numeric key/value is a structural load error.
